title: Miami’s Best Sushi Restaurants
summary: Miami offers no shortage of excellent places to satisfy a sushi craving, whether you’re looking for traditional sushi counters, omakase destinations, Nikkei restaurants, or inventive newcomers. These are the city’s standout sushi spots for a quick lunch, a splurge-worthy dinner,…
whatHappening: Eater Miami has compiled a comprehensive guide to the city's best sushi restaurants, spanning traditional counters, high-end omakase experiences, Nikkei fusion spots, and innovative newcomers. The guide covers options for various occasions, from casual lunch spots to upscale dinner destinations.
whoInvolved: Eater Miami, a leading dining publication covering the South Florida food scene, has curated this list of sushi establishments ranging from established fine dining institutions to emerging restaurants pushing culinary boundaries.
whyMatters: Miami's sushi landscape reflects the city's evolution into a sophisticated dining hub that attracts both culinary talent and discerning diners seeking world-class Japanese cuisine. This guide helps locals and visitors navigate an increasingly competitive and diverse sushi market, influencing dining choices and restaurant discovery in a crowded marketplace.
moreContext: Miami has experienced significant growth in its Japanese dining scene over the past decade, driven by increased international tourism, a wealthy demographic interested in premium dining, and an influx of skilled Japanese chefs opening restaurants in South Florida. The sushi category specifically has diversified beyond traditional offerings to include Nikkei (Japanese-Peruvian fusion), modern interpretations, and omakase-focused establishments that command premium prices. This expansion reflects broader national trends toward experiential dining and chef-driven restaurants, with omakase becoming a status dining experience in major metropolitan areas. Eater's guide serves as an important resource as Miami continues to position itself as a destination for high-end culinary tourism.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour in Miami Beach is establishing itself as a premier destination for locals and visitors seeking an upscale, wellness-focused lifestyle. The neighborhood is gaining recognition as one of Miami's most vibrant and walkable areas, offering an alternative to the tourist-heavy chaos of nearby Ocean Drive.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is highlighting Sunset Harbour, a neighborhood situated on the bay side of South Beach. The area attracts wellness-conscious residents and lifestyle-oriented businesses that prioritize local culture over mass tourism.
whyMatters: This trend reflects a broader shift in Miami's real estate and hospitality landscape toward neighborhood-driven experiences rather than tourist monocultures. The rise of Sunset Harbour as a desirable location could drive property values and investment in the area while positioning it as a model for sustainable urban neighborhoods in Miami.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour's location on the quieter bay side of South Beach provides geographic separation from Ocean Drive's high-energy tourist corridor. The neighborhood's emphasis on walkability and wellness aligns with post-pandemic migration patterns favoring livable, community-oriented neighborhoods. Miami Beach has increasingly positioned itself as a lifestyle destination beyond nightlife and beaches, with neighborhoods like Sunset Harbour leading this evolution. The area's popularity among locals signals a maturing real estate market where authenticity and lifestyle amenities matter more than proximity to traditional tourist attractions.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' annual restaurant promotion month, is returning this July with its largest roster yet. More than 75 restaurants—from Michelin-recognized fine dining to casual neighborhood spots—are participating across diverse cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with restaurants throughout the upscale Miami neighborhood known for its Mediterranean Revival architecture and dining scene.
whyMatters: The expansion signals strong recovery and growth in the local restaurant industry post-pandemic. The event drives foot traffic to participating establishments, boosts tourism spending, and elevates the city's profile as a culinary destination during a key summer travel month.
moreContext: Taste the Gables is an established annual tradition designed to showcase the city's diverse restaurant community and attract diners to explore new and familiar establishments. The participation of Michelin-recognized restaurants alongside neighborhood favorites demonstrates the breadth of Coral Gables' food scene. The July timing capitalizes on summer travel patterns and tourism to South Florida. The growth from previous years' lineups reflects increased restaurant interest and confidence in the market's dining economy.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + More Eating News: Part 1
summary: Reviews of new restaurant openings including Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood, "Meet me at Milos "spritz and aperitivo special, a Dubai chewy cookie, new top spot for coffee lovers with a Norwegian dessert, first Cuban restaurant to get a Michelin star and a bundle for non alcoholic…
whatHappening: Miami's restaurant scene is experiencing a wave of new openings and accolades, including Casa Tua Cucina in Wynwood, specialty drink venues, and notably, the first Cuban restaurant to earn a Michelin star. The dining landscape is expanding with diverse concepts ranging from upscale Italian to casual coffee shops and non-alcoholic beverage destinations.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is reporting on various restaurant operators and concepts entering the market, including Casa Tua Cucina, Milos (known for spritz and aperitivo offerings), and an unnamed Cuban restaurant that has achieved Michelin star status. These establishments represent both established hospitality brands and emerging dining concepts targeting different segments.
whyMatters: The influx of new restaurants signals Miami's continued growth as a culinary destination and reflects evolving consumer preferences toward diverse cuisines, non-alcoholic options, and specialty dining experiences. The Michelin star recognition for a Cuban restaurant represents a watershed moment for Latin American cuisine visibility in fine dining recognition systems.
moreContext: Miami has positioned itself as a major food city over the past decade, attracting acclaimed chefs and diverse culinary concepts. The expansion into neighborhoods like Wynwood reflects broader urban revitalization trends. The emergence of non-alcoholic beverage destinations and specialty coffee spots indicates shifting consumer preferences toward health-conscious dining. Michelin's recognition of Cuban cuisine marks a significant validation for a traditionally underrepresented culinary tradition in formal fine dining hierarchies.

title: A Traditional Italian Fave Made Even Better: Il Mulino
summary: Il Mulino New York located at Acqualina Resort, the Five Star beachfront property which recently celebrated its 20th anniversary, has always been one of my favorite traditional Italian restaurants in Miami. It’s authentic, gives good quality/value, the food is reliably very…
whatHappening: Il Mulino New York, a traditional Italian restaurant located at the Acqualina Resort in Miami, continues to be recognized as a standout dining destination. The restaurant maintains its reputation for authentic Italian cuisine with strong quality-to-value offerings.
whoInvolved: Il Mulino New York operates within Acqualina Resort, a five-star beachfront luxury property in Miami that recently marked its 20th anniversary. The restaurant is part of the renowned Il Mulino brand known for traditional Italian fine dining.
whyMatters: Il Mulino's sustained excellence matters for Miami's dining scene as it represents reliable, high-quality traditional Italian cuisine in a competitive luxury restaurant market. The restaurant's longevity and consistent performance indicate strong customer loyalty and the enduring appeal of authentic Italian dining experiences in South Florida.
moreContext: Il Mulino New York is positioned within Acqualina Resort, one of Miami's premier luxury beachfront properties that has operated for two decades. The restaurant has built a strong reputation among diners for delivering authentic Italian food with dependable quality standards. Traditional Italian fine dining remains a competitive category in Miami, where numerous upscale restaurants vie for discerning diners. Il Mulino's presence at a five-star resort property gives it access to both resort guests and the broader Miami dining community seeking elevated traditional Italian experiences.

title: Miami’s New Modern Mexican Hotspot—What You Need to Know
summary: We went to the Mexican Miami less than three weeks after opening and it was buzzy and many of its seats taken with individuals at the bar, small groups at high top round tables, and others headed to the private rooms celebrating a special occasion. The word has gotten out…
whatHappening: A new modern Mexican restaurant has opened in Miami and is already generating significant buzz just three weeks into operation. The venue is drawing crowds across various dining formats, from bar seating to high-top tables and private dining spaces for celebrations.
whoInvolved: Mexican Miami is the establishment behind this new hotspot in the Miami dining scene. The restaurant appears to be capitalizing on Miami's growing appetite for elevated Mexican cuisine and experiential dining.
whyMatters: The rapid popularity signals strong consumer demand for modern takes on Mexican food in Miami's competitive restaurant market. The success could influence future dining concepts in the area and attract additional investment in upscale Latin cuisine establishments.
moreContext: Miami has seen increased interest in elevated Mexican dining over the past several years as the city's food scene has matured beyond casual fare. The venue's design featuring diverse seating arrangements—from communal bar areas to private dining rooms—reflects current hospitality trends prioritizing flexible spaces for different occasions. Early full seats and word-of-mouth momentum suggest the restaurant has successfully differentiated itself in a crowded market. The three-week benchmark is notable as it indicates the concept has moved beyond opening-week curiosity to establishing sustained traction among diners.
